block_00: Tips to attract leads and build authority with videos
block_02: 82% of traffic is driven by video — so, this channel should be used to the fullest. If for some reason you're still not using videos in your campaigns, here's what you're missing out on:
block_03: Engagement. People spent 2.6x more time on pages with video, which improves on-page and bounce rates.
block_04: Traffic. Video marketing increases website traffic from search engines by 41%.
block_05: Conversions. 78% of marketers report an increase in conversions after implementing video marketing. That’s because an image is always remembered better than a text.
block_07: These types of video content work best in affiliate marketing:
block_08: Reviews — cover the properties of the company's product and their use, advantages, and characteristics.
block_09: Video explainers — teach the viewer how to complete a common task in the product (withdraw, trade options, etc.).
block_10: Testimonials — feature a customer explaining why they use and love the product, i.e., help build trust through social proof.
block_11: Webinars and experts’ talks — help gain the trust of potential customers and boost interaction.
block_13: Here’s how you can add videos with maximum return.
block_15: Are you trying to drive awareness, traffic, conversions, or all of the above? To boost exposure, create top-of-funnel educational videos that build trust. If your goal is to increase conversions, look at creating a product video you can run as an ad on Facebook or YouTube.
block_16: Determine where your video should live
block_17: Who are you targeting? What channels are they most likely to be on? Pick a platform based on how its typical audience and video restrictions align with your content goals:
block_18: YouTube — long-form, evergreen videos like product demos, Q&As, webinars, explainers;
block_19: TikTok — best for short-form videos that will entertain, educate, or inform people with the potential to go viral.
block_20: Instagram (Reels, Lives, IGTVs, and Stories) — the best engagement, discoverability, and lead generation out of any other social platform. Perfect for capturing the attention of an already engaged audience.
block_21: Facebook — product videos, user-generated content, tutorials.
block_23: If you create a video by yourself, make a plan that covers the necessary resources (filming equipment, props, etc.), the overall budget, time for creation, and posting schedule. Alternatively, you can outsource the production or choose a ready-made video from our free library.
block_25: Improve your video marketing with analytics and data
block_26: Pull analytics on your already-published content from the platform you’ll be posting videos on. Different platforms have their own algorithms — they're hidden, but you can try to "hack" them (some pay attention to watch time, others to comments, subscribers, etc.). Regularly look at your videos’ performance metrics to see how you can further improve your marketing.
block_27: Next time, we’ll cover TikTok promotion — stay tuned!
